name: sqlformat
title: sqlformat
description:
SQL formatting utility
The sqlformat command-line tool can be used to reformat SQL file according to
specified options or prepare a snippet in some programming language (only
Python and PHP currently supported).

arguments.description: Change case of identifiers. FORMAT is one of "upper", "lower", "capitalize".
arguments.raw: -i  Change case of identifiers. FORMAT is one of "upper", "lower", "capitalize".

arguments.description: Change case of keywords. FORMAT is one of "upper", "lower", "capitalize".
arguments.raw: -k  Change case of keywords. FORMAT is one of "upper", "lower", "capitalize".

arguments.description: Output a snippet in programming language LANG. LANG can be "python", "php".
arguments.raw: -l  Output a snippet in programming language LANG. LANG can be "python", "php".

arguments.description: Write output to FILE (defaults to stdout).
arguments.raw: -o  Write output to FILE (defaults to stdout).

arguments.description: Print program's version number and exit.
arguments.raw: --version  Print program's version number and exit.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use sqlformat because SQL formatting utility.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use sqlformat as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sqlformat [ OPTION ] ... [ FILE ] ...` as documented upstream for sqlformat.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair sqlformat with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `sqlformat --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -k
arguments.description: Change case of keywords. FORMAT is one of "upper", "lower", "capitalize".

arguments.name: -l

arguments.description: Print program's version number and exit.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: SQL formatting utility.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: SQL formatting utility.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install sqlparse
